title: Single-wire electrode array
abstract: Tissue-stimulating prostheses that include a single-wire electrode array for delivery of electrical stimulation signals (current stimulation) to a recipient. The single-wire electrode array comprises a plurality of electrode devices that are all connected to a stimulator unit via a single-wire connection (i.e., only one wire or lead). Each of the plurality of electrode devices is individually addressable by the stimulator unit for delivery of the current signals to the recipient via the single-wire connection.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A tissue-stimulating prosthesis system, comprising: a stimulator unit configured to generate, based on at least one of the one or more processed signals, current signals for delivery to a recipient; and a single-wire electrode array comprising a plurality of electrode devices all connected to the stimulator unit via a single wire connection, wherein the plurality of electrode devices are individually addressable by the stimulator unit via the single wire connection for delivery of the current signals to the recipient. 2. The tissue-stimulating prosthesis system of claim 1, wherein the electrode devices are individually addressable via the single wire connection using commands encoded with a unique code set for each of the electrode devices. 3. The tissue-stimulating prosthesis system of claim 2, wherein each of the electrode devices comprises a local control unit that will only act upon commands that are tagged with the unique code associated with the corresponding electrode device. 4. The tissue-stimulating prosthesis system of claim 2, wherein each of the electrode devices comprises an electrode switch configured to permit current to flow through the associated electrode device, and wherein the commands include timing information indicating when an electrode switch should be opened to permit current. 5. The tissue-stimulating prosthesis system of claim 1, wherein the stimulator unit is configured to simultaneously deliver the current signals to the recipient via two or more of the electrode devices. 6. The tissue-stimulating prosthesis system of claim 5, wherein each of the electrode devices comprises at least two configurable current sources and a current controller such that 

 a controlled fraction of the current signals can be directed through the two or more of the electrode devices simultaneously in the same direction. 7. The tissue-stimulating prosthesis system of claim 1, wherein the stimulator unit is configured to simultaneously deliver the current signals to the recipient via two or more of the electrode devices such that current flows through a first of the two or more electrode devices in a first direction and current flows through a second one of the two or more electrode devices in a second direction, wherein the second direction is a current flow direction that is opposite to the first direction. 8. The tissue-stimulating prosthesis system of claim 1, further comprising: one or more sound input elements configured to receive sound signals; and a sound processor configured to generate one or more processed signals representative of the sound signals, wherein the stimulator unit is configured to generate the current signals based on at least one of the one or more processed signals, and wherein the current signals are configured to evoke perception of the sound signals by the recipient. 9. The tissue-stimulating prosthesis system of claim 8, wherein the tissue-stimulating prosthesis is a cochlear implant. 10. A tissue-stimulating prosthesis system, comprising: a plurality of electrode devices implanted in a recipient; an implantable power supply; a stimulator unit configured to generate current signals for delivery to the recipient; and only a single wire connection electrically connecting all of the electrode devices to the stimulator unit. 11. The tissue-stimulating prosthesis system of claim 10, wherein the plurality of electrode devices are individually addressable by the stimulator unit via the single wire connection for delivery of the current signals to the recipient. 12. The tissue-stimulating prosthesis system of claim 11, wherein the electrode devices are individually addressable via the single wire connection using commands encoded with a unique code set for each of the electrode devices. 13. The tissue-stimulating prosthesis system of claim 12, wherein each of the electrode devices comprises a local control unit that will only act upon commands that are tagged with the unique code associated with the corresponding electrode device. 14. The tissue-stimulating prosthesis system of claim 12, wherein each of the electrode devices comprises an electrode switch configured to permit current to flow through the associated electrode device, and wherein the commands include timing information indicating when an electrode switch should be opened to permit current. 15. The tissue-stimulating prosthesis system of claim 10, wherein the stimulator unit is configured to simultaneously deliver the current signals to the recipient via two or more of the electrode devices. 16. The tissue-stimulating prosthesis system of claim 15, wherein each of the electrode devices comprises at least two configurable current sources and a current controller such that a controlled fraction of the current signals can be directed through the two or more of the electrode devices simultaneously in the same direction. 17. The tissue-stimulating prosthesis system of claim 10, wherein the stimulator unit is configured to simultaneously deliver the current signals to the recipient via two or more of the electrode devices such that current flows through a first of the two or more electrode devices in a first direction and current flows through a second one of the two or more electrode devices in a second direction, wherein the second direction is a current flow direction that is opposite to the first direction. 18. A method, compri sing : at a stimulator unit of a tissue-stimulating prosthesis system implantable in a recipient, generating electrical stimulation signals; and 

 delivering the electrical stimulation signals to a recipient of the tissue-stimulating prosthesis system via a single-wire electrode array comprising a plurality of electrode devices all connected to the stimulator unit via a single wire connection. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein delivering the electrical stimulation signals to the recipient via the single-wire electrode array comprises: simultaneously delivering the electrical stimulation signals via two or more of the electrode devices in the same direction. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein delivering the electrical stimulation signals to the recipient via the single-wire electrode array comprises: simultaneously deliver the current signals to the recipient via two or more of the electrode devices such that current flows through a first of the two or more electrode devices in a first direction and current flows through a second one of the two or more electrode devices in a second direction, wherein the second direction is a current flow direction that is opposite to the first direction. 21. The method of claim 18, comprising: receiving sound signals at one or more sound input elements of the tissue-stimulating prosthesis system; and processing the sound signal to generate control signals representative of the sound signals, wherein the stimulator unit is configured to generate the electrical stimulation signals based on the control signals.
